stories.headline: Senate impeachment court orders Sara Duterte to appear at July 6 trial opening
stories.summary: The Senate impeachment court has directed Vice President Sara Duterte to appear when her trial opens Monday, July 6, at 2 p.m. at the Senate in Pasay City; she may attend in person or through counsel. Senate officials said the chamber is ready to begin, and Malacanang urged Duterte to attend in person. The court earlier approved a 92-day trial period, allotting 62 hearing dates to the House prosecution, which listed 57 witnesses, and 30 to the defense, which named 90.
stories.context: The four articles of impeachment cover alleged misuse of confidential funds, unexplained wealth, bribery involving education officials, and threats against President Marcos, First Lady Marie Louise Araneta-Marcos and former House Speaker Ferdinand Martin Romualdez.
stories.watch_for: The trial opens Monday, July 6, at 2 p.m. and is scheduled to run Mondays to Wednesdays until President Marcos's State of the Nation Address on July 27.

stories.headline: Ombudsman files plunder charges against Senator Marcoleta
stories.summary: The Office of the Ombudsman filed plunder charges before the Sandiganbayan against Senator Rodante Marcoleta and three others - former congressman Mike Defensor and businessmen Joseph Espiritu and Aristotle Viray - over 75 million pesos in campaign donations Marcoleta allegedly received in January 2025 but did not declare in his campaign expenditure statement or asset disclosure. Marcoleta said the case aims to "intimidate independent voices" and vowed not to be silenced.

stories.headline: Philippines and Canada elevate ties to strategic partnership, sign cooperation deals
stories.summary: President Marcos and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney announced the elevation of Philippines-Canada relations to a "strategic partnership" during talks in Vancouver, and oversaw the signing of agreements covering energy and natural resources, tourism and culture, and labor and migration. The two leaders said they aim to conclude a bilateral free trade agreement by the end of 2026.
stories.context: The visit marked the first by a Philippine head of state to Canada in 11 years, and comes as the two countries also work toward a wider Canada-ASEAN free trade agreement.
stories.watch_for: The two countries' foreign ministries were tasked with drafting a Plan of Action to build on the agreements, including support for a rules-based order in the South China Sea.

stories.headline: World Bank reclassifies Philippines as upper-middle income country
stories.summary: The World Bank said the Philippines has reached upper-middle-income status, with gross national income per capita reaching a record $4,850 in 2025, placing it within the $4,636 to $14,375 threshold. The country had been classified as lower-middle income since 1987; the change reflects average GDP growth of 5.8 percent annually over the past five years.
stories.context: The World Bank updates income classifications every July 1 using the prior year's gross national income data; the Philippines was one of five countries, along with Jordan, Micronesia, Sri Lanka and Vietnam, to move up a tier this year.

stories.headline: Philippine banks' bad loan ratio hits nine-month high
stories.summary: The gross non-performing loan ratio of Philippine banks widened to 3.44 percent as of end-May, the highest in nine months, up from 3.37 percent in April, as elevated borrowing costs strained some borrowers. The peso value of soured loans rose 3.7 percent from April to 601.4 billion pesos. Analyst Jonathan Ravelas called it "a caution flag rather than a red flag," saying the banking system remains fundamentally sound.

stories.headline: Competition watchdog fines Boracay dive shops for price fixing
stories.summary: The Philippine Competition Commission fined the Boracay Business Administration of Scuba Shops and 39 member shops a combined 2.17 million pesos for fixing prices on diving services, including minimum rates for certification courses and capped commissions, in violation of the Philippine Competition Act. The commission rejected the group's argument that the price floors protected consumer safety and local livelihoods.

stories.headline: DepEd, PNP push new measures amid rise in school violence, threats
stories.summary: Education Secretary Sonny Angara urged schools nationwide to strengthen coordination with Parent-Teacher Associations to help prevent campus violence, invoking DepEd's 2022 PTA guidelines. Separately, the PNP warned of a rise in school-related threats, saying many appear to involve students trying to avoid classes or exams, and urged the public to verify reports before sharing them online.
stories.context: The push follows a string of recent incidents, including a shooting at a Tacloban City high school on June 22 that killed three students, and a false shooting report at a Quezon City school that circulated online this week.

stories.headline: Alex Eala reaches Wimbledon third round for the first time
stories.summary: Alex Eala rallied from a set down to beat Australia's Maya Joint 3-6, 6-2, 6-0 on July 2, becoming the first Filipino to reach the third round of a Grand Slam singles tournament in the Open era. The 29th seed avenged a loss to Joint in last year's Eastbourne Open final.
stories.watch_for: Eala faces defending Wimbledon champion Iga Swiatek in the third round on Saturday, July 4.

stories.headline: Super typhoon Bavi intensifies further outside PAR, could be named 'Inday'
stories.summary: PAGASA said Super Typhoon Bavi continued to strengthen while remaining outside the Philippine Area of Responsibility, with maximum sustained winds of 195 km/h and gusts up to 240 km/h as of the morning of July 4, when it was about 2,985 kilometers east of Eastern Visayas and moving west. Forecasters said Bavi may enter the PAR by Wednesday, July 8, when it would be given the local name "Inday."
stories.watch_for: PAGASA said wind signals could later be raised over Northern and possibly Central Luzon and that Bavi may enhance the southwest monsoon; separately, easterlies were already bringing rain to parts of Cagayan Valley, Bicol and Eastern Visayas, with a risk of flash floods and landslides.

stories.headline: Russian missile and drone strike on Kyiv kills at least 22
stories.summary: Russia launched 74 missiles and 496 drones at Ukraine overnight into July 2, with Kyiv the main target. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted most of them, but 25 ballistic missiles and 12 drones struck 33 locations across the capital, killing at least 22 people and wounding dozens, including children. President Zelenskyy said Russia had "deliberately attacked ordinary civilians and residential buildings."
stories.context: Kyiv's mayor called it the war's most massive attack on the capital, with a nine-story residential building among the sites hit. Russia's defense ministry said the strikes were carried out in response to Ukrainian drone attacks inside Russia.

stories.headline: US hiring slows sharply in June, payrolls rise by just 57,000
stories.summary: US employers added 57,000 jobs in June, well below the roughly 110,000 to 115,000 economists had forecast, while the unemployment rate edged down to 4.2 percent as labor force participation fell to its lowest level since March 2021. Leisure and hospitality shed 61,000 jobs despite World Cup-related tourism, while professional services, healthcare and social assistance posted gains.
stories.context: The Bureau of Labor Statistics also revised down April and May payroll gains by a combined 74,000 jobs, adding to signs the labor market has cooled significantly this year.

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll climbs to 2,645 with thousands missing
stories.summary: The death toll from the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela's La Guaira area on June 24 has risen to 2,645, with about 38,500 people still missing, National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez said, and officials expect it to climb further. International teams and residents were still searching the rubble; a 43-year-old man was pulled out alive on July 2, eight days after the magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 quakes.
stories.context: The government ordered 10,000 body bags and set up tent camps for thousands of displaced people, according to the United Nations and local officials; experts have cautioned that finding more survivors is increasingly unlikely at this stage.

stories.headline: PNP to deploy about 6,000 officers to secure Senate for impeachment trial
stories.summary: The Philippine National Police said it will deploy about 6,000 officers around the Senate in Pasay City for the July 6 opening of Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial. PNP spokesperson Col. Allen Rae Co said a comprehensive security plan is in place and intelligence monitoring is ongoing, with operations focused on the Senate premises.
stories.watch_for: The security deployment takes effect for the trial opening on Monday, July 6.

stories.headline: PSEi ends the week higher as oil prices ease
stories.summary: The Philippine Stock Exchange index closed at 6,188.03 on Friday, July 3, up 62.31 points or 1.02 percent, as investors picked up bargains and global oil prices fell. Mining stocks led the gains on higher gold prices, with advancers outnumbering decliners 108 to 64. Trading value was subdued at about 4.44 billion pesos.

stories.headline: Young Pangasinan town councilor dies in vehicle crash
stories.summary: A 25-year-old municipal councilor in Bayambang, Pangasinan, Jan Nazer David Junio, died in a single-vehicle crash late on July 2, police said. Investigators said Junio, the town council's presiding officer pro tempore, was driving at high speed when his vehicle struck a bridge barrier in San Carlos City; he was taken to a hospital in Dagupan and declared dead. Police said the investigation is continuing.

stories.headline: Gilas Pilipinas falls to New Zealand in double-overtime qualifier
stories.summary: Gilas Pilipinas lost to New Zealand 106-102 in double overtime in a FIBA World Cup 2027 Asian Qualifier in Auckland on July 3. Juan Gomez de Liano and Kevin Quiambao scored 23 points each for the Philippines, with Gomez de Liano hitting a three-pointer late in regulation to force the first overtime. The defeat dropped Gilas to a 2-3 record in the first round.

stories.headline: Iran begins seven-day state funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei
stories.summary: Iran began a seven-day state funeral on July 3 for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who Iranian state media and international outlets say was killed in a US-Israeli air strike on his compound on February 28 during the war between Iran and Israel. Al Jazeera reported that public ceremonies in Tehran will be followed by processions toward Qom, a reception in Najaf, Iraq, and burial at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad on July 9.
stories.context: Representatives from more than 100 countries are expected to attend, including heads of government from Pakistan, Tajikistan, Armenia and Georgia and senior delegations from Turkey, India, China and Russia; organizers expect crowds surpassing the 1989 funeral of Ayatollah Khomeini.

stories.headline: Foreign investors buy record $60 billion of Japanese stocks in first half
stories.summary: Foreign investors bought a net 9.7 trillion yen, about $60 billion, of Japanese stocks in the first half of 2026, a record for any half-year, Nikkei Asia reported. It attributed the inflows to improving corporate capital efficiency and strong interest in chip- and AI-related companies. The Nikkei Stock Average has risen about 39 percent since the end of 2025.
